The contact owns a 2020 Kia Forte. The contact stated while driving approximately 30 MPH, the Forward Collision Avoidance camera activated, causing the brakes to stop the vehicle unintendedly. The Forward Collision Avoidance system activated without a vehicle or person nearby. Th

narrative.headline: 2020 Kia Forte: power train leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 69 complaints with NHTSA. The power train is the most reported area at 22%, followed closely by the engine (20%) and electrical system (16%). No deaths are reported. Air bags draw only 4 reports (6%) but account for all 4 crashes and 5 injuries in this data, so they are worth a close look.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 15 reports (22%), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. Watch for the car barely accelerating when you press the gas.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 14 reports (20%), including 1 with a fire. No crashes or injuries reported here.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 11 reports (16%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. A recurring complaint area worth checking on a test drive.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Only 4 reports (6%) but the most serious by outcome: all 4 involve a crash and together they account for 5 injuries.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 4 reports (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe the system braking on its own with nothing ahead.
